id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biography of the user.

socials.description: Links to the user's social media profiles.

schoolCode.description: The code for the user's school or enterprise.

onboarded.description: Whether the user has completed the initial profile setup.

phone.description: The user's contact phone number for deliveries.

Book.title: Book
Book.description: A book written by an author.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Book entity.

status.description: The status of the book (draft or published).

coverUrl.description: URL of the book cover image.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the book was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp when the book was last updated.

ratingAvg.description: The average rating of the book.

ratingCount.description: The number of ratings for the book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Chapter entity.

order.description: The order of the chapter within the book.

wordCount.description: The word count of the chapter.

illustrations.description: Array of illustration URLs for the chapter.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: A user review for a book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

rating.description: The rating given by the user for the book.

comment.description: The comment provided by the user for the book.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the review was created.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: An order placed by a user for a book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

amount.description: The amount paid for the order.

timestamp.description: The timestamp when the order was placed.

AIJob.title: AIJob
AIJob.description: A job for the AI to perform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AIJob entity.

payload.description: The payload for the AI job.

status.description: The status of the AI job.

resultUrl.description: The URL of the result of the AI job.

Flag.title: Flag
Flag.description: A flag for content moderation or AI-generated feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Flag entity.

type.description: The type of content being flagged (e.g., book, review).

contentId.description: The ID of the content being flagged or reviewed.

title.description: A title for the feedback or flag.

reason.description: The reason for flagging the content or the AI-generated critique.

date.description: The date when the content was flagged.

status.description: The status of the flag (e.g., pending, resolved).

SchoolCode.title: SchoolCode
SchoolCode.description: A code for a school or enterprise.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SchoolCode entity.

schoolName.description: The name of the school or enterprise.

params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the Gemini Books application's features, emphasizing authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. It leverages path-based ownership for user-specific data and avoids hierarchical authorization dependencies by denormalizing authorization context where necessary.

*   **Users Collection (`/users/{userId}`):** Stores user profiles. Authorization: Only the user with the matching `userId` can read/write their own document.
*   **Books Collection (`/books/{bookId}`):** Stores all books in a single top-level collection. This is a more scalable approach than nesting books under users. The `authorId` field is indexed to allow for efficient and secure querying of books by a specific author. Authorization for writes is based on `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId`.
*   **Chapters Subcollection (`/books/{bookId}/chapters/{chapterId}`):** Chapters are nested under books. This maintains data locality and improves query performance for fetching a book's content.
*   **Reviews Collection (`/reviews/{reviewId}`):** A top-level collection for all reviews. This allows for flexible querying, such as fetching all reviews by a user or for a specific book, using indexes on `userId` and `bookId`.
*   **Orders Collection (`/orders/{orderId}`):** A top-level collection for purchase orders, queryable by `userId`.
*   **AI Jobs Collection (`/ai_jobs/{jobId}`):** A top-level collection for managing background AI tasks.
*   **Flags Collection (`/flags/{flagId}`):** A top-level collection for content moderation and AI feedback, allowing admins to query all pending flags easily.
*   **School Codes Collection (`/schoolCodes/{schoolCodeId}`):** Stores school codes for validation.
*   **Admin Roles Collection (`/roles_admin/{userId}`):** A simple and secure way to manage admin privileges via document existence.

This flattened structure simplifies security rules, improves query flexibility, and is the standard best practice for scalable Firestore design.
